see3ducks said

There is a 'Reset Folder Order' option in TB 149, but it hasn't been back-ported to 140 and not sure it will be.

If you might want to upgrade to release...

Release Channel:

- more stable

- newest features

- other improvements, e.g., graphical/functional

Note: some add-ons will not work until they are updated

Extended Support Release Channel (ESR):

- stability

- consistent behavior

- low maintenance

- fewer new features

See more details:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/choosing-thunderbird-release-channel/

https://support.mozilla.org/kb/thunderbird-release/

content:
I suggest your first priority be to establish a backup plan now. If you wait until you possibly restore everything, you may lose the urgency of preventing a future issue. Software always crashes into oblivion without advance notice. And check that the restore works. A common problem I see are users with backups that, when restored, do not work.

Now, if the account is IMAP, log onto your online account. That will give you immediate confirmation on whether your messages are lost. if POP account, I suggest these steps:

  1. click help>troubleshootinginformation
  2. scroll down left side to 'profiles' and click 'about:profiles'
  3. if there is more than one proflle, click to open the one not in use, and click the Mail folder and the then POP account folder. My hope is that thunderbird upped to another profile and the POP account is still there. If unsure, post screenshots of what you see there. What you want to see are files such as inbox, inbox.msf, sent, sent.msf, etc.

I am including a same backup strategy here:


BACKUP

  1. ensure thunderbird is not running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media

RESTORE

  1. ensure thunderbird is installed and NOT running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. delete the thunderbird folder
  4. you should be in Appdata\roaming folder , copy&paste the exported Thunderbird folder there.
  5. start thunderbird and all should be there
creator: davidsk

content:
Well, there are sharper tools in this shed than I, but I will assist where I can. I understand the frustration.

First, you need to have the ability to create screenshots in JPG or PNG format that can be uploaded here. Windows has the ability to do that, but my fingers always stumble with that tool, and I prefer a separate, stand-alone program for screenshots: Here are two FREE ones that are relatively easy to use. The steps in using either of them is to 1) start the utility, then 2) switch view to the screen where you want to capture part of the screen, then 3) switch back to the utility and press the 'capture' button, and then 4) drag the mouse to capture a rectangle area and save it. https://www.reasyze.com/ look for 'sniptool' My favorite, because it easily lets one add arrows or text, is at https://portableapps.com/apps/graphics_pictures/fscportable

Next, do this:

  1. click the three-line menu in upper right (often called the hamburger menu) and select 'account settings'
  2. highlight the account in left side bar to ensure all screenshots are for that account
  3. now, click 'server settings' in left sidebar and do a screenshot of the right side of screen.
  4. now exit back to main Thunderbird screen
  5. next, click the help>troubleshooting menu item
  6. scroll down to 'profiles' and click 'about:profiles'
  7. do a screenshot there.
  8. post back with both screenshots

I infer that you logged on to website, checked email there, and found no messages in sent folder or in inbox. Please confirm. Thank you. I await your response. AND, some others may respond also to this thread, so you may get further assistance.

You can drag-and-drop folders within an account's hierarchy to whatever order you want. But be careful! - it's a delicate operation and it's easy to accidentally drop a folder into another folder rather than between them.

As of Thunderbird version 149 there's a new option to "Reset Folder Order" which will automatically rearrange them in alphabetical order.

content:
Delimiters, which are punctuation or spaces on either side of a string of characters, are used generate search terms. For example, a message which contains 

123.456.987 000

will cause search terms to be indexed for "123", "456" and "987". For the purposes of indexing, neither the type of punctuation used (comma, period, question mark, etc) nor its placement matters. Likewise, the search process is agnostic to the punctuation used, so searching on any of the following will find the example message above: 

456.987 456!987. "456!987."

Terms of one or two characters are not indexed. The minimum term that Thunderbird will index is three characters. So, a message containing the following will not have "4" nor "91" indexed: 

4.91.456.987

As a result, attempts to search on "4" or "91" will not find any messages. Also, adding a short string like "22" or "32" to other search terms will not affect the search results - these short strings are in effect ignored. "22 456" will find the example message above, even though "22" is not in the message.

content:
This is a bit tedious, but it works. It does NOT work, though, if you have compacted trash since deleting.

  1. click help>troubleshootinginformation
  2. scroll to 'profile folders' and click 'open folder'
  3. exit thunderbird
  4. you are now in the profile. The trash folder will either be in the Imapmail folder, the Mail folder, or the Mail\Local Folders folder. Look for the trash, trash msf entries. IGNORE the msf file.
  5. Open the file "Trash" with Notepad or a similar text editor.
  6. Locate the line before your email text that reads "X-Mozilla-Status: 000X" (where 'X' is any character other than 0). Change the number to four zeros (″0000"). Repeat for all other messages. Save the document and exit Notepad
  7. restart thunderbird and the messages should appear. Let me know how it goes. Please.
creator: davidsk

content:
david said
This is a bit tedious, but it works.

david, as I said in the first sentence of my answer: this doesn't work if the Trash folder has been emptied via R-click "Empty Trash" since Trash (or Deleted) folders are automatically compacted after the 'Empty Trash' command. Your method only works if Jean has not used the 'Empty Trash' command but only deleted messages from the Trash folder by selecting them and using the "Delete Selected Messages" command in the context menu

content:
Probabilmente nella tua rubrica hai associato il nome di questo mittente all'indirizzo di un provider PEC. Dovrebbe bastare cancellare il mittente dalla rubrica:

La PEC nel sistema italiano è una particolare email con valore legale. Per ottenere questo risultato la comunicazione tra un mittente MITT ed un destinatario DEST è mediata dal una società abilitata alla gestione della PEC rispondente a precisi requisiti tecnici (GEST). Nelle intestazioni della PEC avremo quindi riferimenti a MITT, DEST e GEST. Supponiamo che DEST, ricevuta una PEC da MITT, voglia aggiungere il mittente alla propria rubrica di Thunderbird. A volte capita che al nome di MITT non vanga associato il relativo indirizzo email ma quello dell'intermediario GEST. Il risultato è che da quel momento in poi tutte le PEC ricevute da utenti di GEST appariranno con mittente MIT, talvolta con la dicitura "per conto di". Per risolvere il problema è necessario cancellare il contatto MITT dalla rubrica.

content:
There is no practical limit to how many accounts you can have in Thunderbird, or if there is, it's quite high. I have eight. How are you trying to add the third account? What error messages do you see?

Normally, you just go to <Accounts Settings | + New Account | Mail Account> and you will be led through the process by Thunderbird's Wizard. See the attached image.

Thunderbird has a new wizard called "Account Hub". It is labeled "Experimental" - it works fine for most people, but if it doesn't for you, it can easily be disabled. That setting is found at <Settings | General | Account Hub>. Uncheck the option to disable.
